newsSince: Genuinely new since June 9: Equinix agreed to a deal where CPP Investments invests ~US$1.6B for ~60% of atNorth (Equinix retains ~40%), funding rapid scaling of Nordic, liquid-cooling-enabled AI/HPC capacity (8 operational DCs, ~1 GW secured power); described as immediately AFFO-accretive on close. Supports the AI data-center deployment thesis. Stock +5.9% since June 9.

sources.label: Simply Wall St: Equinix valuation after raised guidance on AI DC demand
Equinix 8-K: Gemini/atNorth (CPP Investments JV) press release
Barchart: Equinix becoming a key stop in the AI supply chain

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing new since June 9 — guidance raise, atNorth JV and Citi Focus List add were already in the prior case. On this under-owned-bottleneck list the continued +5.9% rerating (now +47% YTD) further erodes the residual alpha the page rewards, so slight negative drift; hold rank 66.
verdicts.bull: ~60% of largest deals AI-tied, raised FY26 guide and atNorth high-density expansion entrench colocation scarcity.
verdicts.neutral: Quality bottleneck but widely owned and richly valued; power/capacity constraints cut both ways.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap REIT at +47% YTD; under-owned thesis largely exhausted; expansion-cost drag.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI colocation bookings; xScale/atNorth capacity adds
